title: Saucy Chicken & Bell Pepper Stir-Fry with Rice
description: EveryPlate's popular weeknight stir-fry — tender chicken strips and colorful bell peppers tossed in a savory, slightly sweet soy-ginger sauce and served over fluffy rice. On the table in under 30 minutes.

ingredients.item: Boneless skinless chicken breasts or thighs

instructions: Cook rice according to package directions.
Slice chicken into thin strips. Toss with cornstarch, a pinch of salt, and pepper.
Slice bell peppers and onion into strips. Mince garlic and ginger. Slice scallions.
Make the sauce: whisk together soy sauce, oyster sauce, hoisin sauce, and sesame oil. Set aside.
Heat 1 tbsp vegetable oil in a large wok or skillet over high heat until smoking. Add chicken in a single layer; cook 2–3 minutes per side until golden and cooked through. Remove to a plate.
Add remaining oil to the pan. Add onion and bell peppers; stir-fry 3–4 minutes until slightly softened but still crisp.
Add garlic and ginger; stir-fry 30 seconds until fragrant.
Return chicken to the pan. Pour sauce over everything and toss to coat. Cook 1–2 minutes until sauce thickens and coats the chicken and vegetables.
Serve over rice, garnished with scallions and sesame seeds.
tips: High heat is the key to a good stir-fry — make sure your pan is very hot before adding anything.
Tossing the chicken in cornstarch before cooking gives it a light coating that helps the sauce cling and keeps it juicy.
Prep everything before you start cooking — stir-fries move fast.
Add snap peas, broccoli, or baby corn for extra vegetables.
